blocks.text: DTC U0151: F-CAN communication error (PGM-FI ECU-SRS unit communication failure)

The control unit-to-control unit communication (F-CAN) for powertrain/chassis systems uses two communication lines (F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L) to send and receive multiple signals simultaneously between multiple control units. If signals from the SRS unit sent using F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L are not received for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
When the PGM-FI ECU cannot receive signals from the SRS unit sent using F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L for more than 1.5 seconds.

items.text: Disconnection of F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_H line)
Disconnection of F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_L line)
Short circuit in F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_H line)
Short circuit in F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_L line)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
